questions.question_text: You're building an MCP client to connect your application to an MCP server. What are the two main components you need?

options.text: A frontend and a backend
A database and a web server
A REST API and a GraphQL endpoint
An MCP Client class and a Client Session

questions.question_text: Your MCP client needs to find out what tools are available from an MCP server. What message type should it send?

options.text: ListToolsRequest
CallToolRequest
ToolDiscoveryRequest
GetToolsMessage

questions.question_text: You've built an MCP server and want to test if your tools work correctly before connecting to a full application. What's the easiest way to do this?

options.text: Write separate test scripts for each tool
Test everything manually in the terminal
Use the built-in MCP Inspector with `mcp dev mcp_server.py`
Connect directly to Claude first

questions.question_text: You're building a chat app where users ask Claude about their GitHub data. Without MCP, what's the main problem you'd face?

options.text: GitHub doesn't allow API access
You'd have to write and maintain all the GitHub tool code yourself
Claude can't understand GitHub data
Users can't ask questions about repositories

questions.question_text: You're deciding how to implement a new feature in your MCP server. Users should be able to click a button to trigger a 'summarize document' workflow. Which MCP primitive should you use?

options.text: Resources - because you need to fetch document data
Functions - because it involves processing
Prompts - because users control when to start the workflow
Tools - because the AI needs new capabilities

questions.question_text: You're using the Python MCP SDK to create a tool that reads files. What's the easiest way to define this tool?

options.text: Use the @mcp.tool() decorator on a Python function
Create a separate configuration file
Write JSON schemas manually
Send HTTP requests to register the tool

questions.question_text: You want to create a resource that fetches different documents based on their ID, like docs://documents/report.pdf. What type of resource should you use?

options.text: A templated resource with parameters in the URI
A tool instead of a resource
A direct resource with a static URI
A database query resource
